records.ety: [Akin to D. glans luster, brightness, G. glanz, Sw. glans, D. glands brightness, glimpse. Cf. Gleen, Glint, Glitter, and Glance a mineral.]

defs: A sudden flash of light or splendor.
A quick cast of the eyes; a quick or a casual look; a swift survey; a glimpse.
quotes: Dart not scornful glances from those eyes.

defs: An incidental or passing thought or allusion.
quotes: How fleet is a glance of the mind.

defs: A name given to some sulphides, mostly dark-colored, which have a brilliant metallic luster, as the sulphide of copper, called copper glance.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Glanced (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Glancing (?).]

defs: To shoot or emit a flash of light; to shine; to flash.
quotes: From art, from nature, from the schools, Let random influences glance, Like light in many a shivered lance, That breaks about the dappled pools.

defs: To strike and fly off in an oblique direction; to dart aside. ”Your arrow hath glanced”.
quotes: On me the curse aslope Glanced on the ground.

defs: To look with a sudden, rapid cast of the eye; to snatch a momentary or hasty view.
quotes: The poet's eye, in a fine frenzy rolling, Doth glance from heaven to earth, from earth to heaven.

defs: To make an incidental or passing reflection; to allude; to hint; -- often with at.
quotes: Wherein obscurely Caesar's ambition shall be glanced at.
He glanced at a certain reverend doctor.

defs: To move quickly, appearing and disappearing rapidly; to be visible only for an instant at a time; to move interruptedly; to twinkle.
quotes: And all along the forum and up the sacred seat, His vulture eye pursued the trip of those small glancing feet.

defs: To shoot or dart suddenly or obliquely; to cast for a moment; as, to glance the eye.
To hint at; to touch lightly or briefly.
quotes: In company I often glanced it.
